evidence_quotes.quote: Shutting down a game after purchase undermines trust and treats it more like a rental without clearly stating so.
This practice erodes consumer rights and devalues digital ownership.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I'd like to add my 2 cents on this matter. Owning what you paid for is a logical outcome because a purchase implies a transaction of value for lasting access or ownership. When consumers buy a game, they expect the ability to use it indefinitely, not just for as long as the publisher permits. Shutting down a game after purchase undermines trust and treats it more like a rental without clearly stating so. This practice erodes consumer rights and devalues digital ownership. Ultimately, if you pay for a product, you should have the right to access and enjoy it as promised.
